entry.title: Introducing the new "Test Plan Status" filter in Test Executions app in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Running a Focused and Targeted Testing Process

In the world of software development, ensuring that your testing process remains focused and targeted is essential. Unnecessary information like test cases, which are not meant to be tested, shown to the tester can irritate and probably delay the execution. This blog post discusses the new functionality to reduce the test case list in the Test Execution app to only show test cases, which are related to an active test plan.

With this, we build on the already available functionality of using test plans for an advanced test orchestration, which is perfectly described in the related blog post Advanced Test Orchestration and Tester assignment using Test Plans in SAP Cloud ALM. Don’t miss reading it beforehand.

 

Introducing the "Test Plan Status" Filter

To help testers focus on test cases linked to an active test plan, a new filter, “Test Plan Status,” has been added to the Test Execution app. By default, this filter is set to “In Testing,” which does exclude all test cases from the execution list, which are not assigned to a test plan, or which are assigned to a finished test plan.

Along with the new filter, we have moved the “(None)” option in the Test Plan filter to the last position to ensure that customers who exclusively work with Test Plans are not inconvenienced by this entry.

 

How to Use the "Test Plan Status" Filter in Cloud ALM

For those utilizing Cloud ALM for test management, especially if you’re managing test cases only with structured test plans, this feature can be particularly useful. Here’s how you can make the most of it:

  1. Default Filter Setting: By default, the “Test Plan Status” filter is set to “In Testing.” This configuration ensures that testers focus on the cases that require attention without getting sidetracked by those not in progress or which are not associated to a test plan.

  2. Customizing the Filter: If your workflow doesn’t include a formal test plan or if you want a view of all test cases, you can modify the filter. Simply remove the default filter or set it additionally to “(None)” to include all test cases, even those outside the designated test plans.

  3. Saving Your Preferences: Once you adjust the filter settings to your needs, you can save these as a personal view. This setup allows for a customized testing view that aligns with your specific testing requirements and preferences. You can set them also as Default view, which allows a direct filtering for your preferences whenever entering the Test Execution app.

 

Conclusion

A focused testing process can make all the difference in delivering reliable, high-quality software. By leveraging the possibilities of the new “Test Plan Status” filter in Cloud ALM, testers can streamline their efforts and maintain a targeted approach.

entry.title: SAP Community Question: In your opinion, what's the biggest use-case for Blockchain at SAP Customers
entry.content:
Good Morning SAP Community,

in your opinion what is _the_ biggest use case for Enterprise Blockchain / Distributed Ledger Technology at SAP Customers ?

Anybody following my blogs will know I like Enterprise Blockchain, and am interested in positioning Enterprise Blockchain as a Technology Standard for use-cases and business demands at SAP Customers enabling them to run Enterprise Blockchain.

I talk to a lot of people about this and regularly get asked the same question, what is _the_ BIG use case for Blockchain in the Enterprise and at SAP Customers ?

So, the floor is open, the comments are open, there is no wrong answer, and everybody is invited to give their opinion on,

for SAP Customers, what is the biggest use case for Blockchain ?

Over to you, feel welcome to put your thoughts in the comments and let's see what we discover, and, there is no wrong answer 🙂

Andy Silvey.

entry.title: Updated Learning Journey - Exploring the Lead to Cash Business Process in SAP Customer Experience
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community! Fernando at the controls!

Just two years ago in November, we released the Exploring the Lead to Cash Business Process in SAP Customer Experience learning journey.

After about 4,000 learners going over it and more than 1,000 record of achievement badges issued to those who have completed the whole course, we analyzed the feedback given to us, and started a mayor update of the whole course content.

This complete course update comes with a long number of changes:

  • Updated SAP Customer Experience Solutions Portfolio used during the business process.

 

  • Introduced Artificial Intelligence for CX and its use cases during the stages for lead to cash supported by all Customer Experience solutions.

 

  • Included a Business Sample to during all the Lead to Cash stages to visualize the steps.

 

  • Updated the Lead to Cash Process Variants with those already mentioned in SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

 

  • 10 net new units and lessons for all the Lead to Cash content with new videos, diagrams, text descriptions and further learning resources.

So, what are you waiting for? go to SAP Learning site, and enroll for the new updated version of Exploring the Lead to Cash Business Process in SAP Customer Experience, after completing it... Don't forget to claim your digital badge to show it in your professional social network profile!

I’ll be back very soon to present you new learning courses, live sessions, micro learnings and any other educational asset which can be interesting for you related to SAP Customer Experience Integration and Extensibility area!

In the mean time, I’ll be looking forward to your feedback and questions via comments here in this post.

entry.title: Cycle Counting Physical Inventory in SAP WM at Bin Level
entry.content:
Introduction- In cycle counting inventory, you enter your material stocks physically at regular intervals within a fiscal year. By dividing materials into various classes (for example, A, B, C, D, and so on), you specify for those materials at which intervals or cycles the inventory is to be performed. This allows you to make an inventory fast-moving materials in your warehouse more often than slow-moving material.

Cycle counting is basically the process of continually validation the accuracy of the inventory in the warehouse by regularly counting a portion of the inventory on a daily or weekly basis. Every item in the warehouse is counted at lease several times in a year.

Cycle Counting can be performed at IM level as well as WM Level.

In WM  We can go for two types of Cycle Counting Physical Inventory

1- Cycle Counting at Bin Level

2- Cycle Counting at Quant Level 

In this blog I am going to write about Cycle Counting at Bin Level

WM Level Cycle Counting Configuration

  • ABC  indicator has to be maintained in Material Management

Navigation SPRO-Material Management-Inventory Management and Physical Inventory-Physical Inventory-Cycle Counting

Master Data Set UP at IM Level

Set Cycle Counting Indicator (M1BC)

Navigation Logistics – Material Management- Physical Inventory- Special Procedures-Cycle Counting – Set Cycle Counting Indicator

Click change CC Indicator

It can be checked in MM03

 

Cycle Counting Configuration in WM for Storage Type

Navigation SPRO-Logistic Execution- Warehouse Management – Activity – Physical Inventory -Define Types per storage type

Process Flow

Create Cycle Counting Document in WM

Transaction Code LX26

Count the inventory

Transaction code – LI11n

Input Counted quantity In put here lesser than actual

And save the count

Inventory Count Added

Clear difference in WM

Transaction LI20

Write Off the count

Clear difference at IM level

Transaction code LI21 or LX23

I am taking here LI21

After write off  Material document will be created

Check LX03

One pallet quantity was 0 so it disappeared

Other pallet quantity 1 and it was increased by 1 quantity so it is changed

After the entire physical inventory is complete for the fiscal year, you reset the inventory data for storage bins and storage quants using the RLREOLPQ report. In the next inventory period, you can then perform a new physical inventory for these storage bins.

Note- You should not reset the quant data, since the reference to the inventory date is then lost, and the system proposes too many bins for your next cycle counting inventory.

Reference - https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/b2dee5e83e2446149294f9860a7c08f0/8f91c95360267214e10000000a174cb4.html

Please share your feedback so I can improve it 

 

Regards

Rashid

entry.title: Cycle Counting Physical Inventory in SAP WM at Quant Level
entry.content:
Hi All

I wrote blog previously about Cycle Counting at Bin Level https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/cycle-counting-physical-inventory-in-sap-wm-at-bin-level/ba-p/13938236. In this blog I am going to write about Cycle Counting at Quant Level in WM.

Introduction- In contrast to the Cycle Counting Inventory, the cycle counting inventory at quant level is not based on the storage bin, but rather on the individual quants in your warehouse.

If you use cycle counting inventory at quant level, the system does not set the inventory block for the entire storage bin. It sets the block instead for the quant to be inventoried. You count only the selected materials, not all materials in the storage bin. If you manage storage bins with mixed storage, these storage bins are accessible for stock movements with other quants. There can be open transfer orders for all materials that were not flagged for cycle counting in the inventory document. You can also create transfer orders for these materials during the inventory. The system saves the inventory data at quant level.

All Configuration Steps will be same I mentioned in my Previous Blog https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/cycle-counting-physical-inventory-in-sap-wm-at-bin-level/ba-p/13938236 Except below additional configuration,

In Customizing - SPRO- Logistic Execution-Warehouse Management- Activities- Physical Inventory- Define Types per Storage Type ,

 

you have specified the inventory procedure for the required storage type as continuous inventory using cycle counting.

 

SPRO-Logistic Execution- Warehouse Management -Activities- Physical Inventory -Maintain Number Ranges for Inventory number range Quant

Process Step

To create physical inventory documents at quant level for the cycle counting inventory

Logistics Execution- Internal Whse Processes - Physical Inventory - In Warehouse Management Physical Inventory Document - Cycle Counting per Quant
Transaction Code LICC

Note- If you generate inventory documents for the cycle counting inventory at quant level, these documents are active immediately . In this case, you cannot schedule the physical inventory in advance by first generating the inventory documents and activating them at a later point.The system immediately blocks the affected quants for all other stock movements.

For Rest Process Step we can refer last Blog https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/cycle-counting-physical-inventory-in-sap-wm-at-bin-level/ba-p/13938236

Count LI11N

WM Level Difference LI20

IM Level Difference LI21 or LX23

To obtain an evaluation about the progress of the cycle counting

Logistics Execution  Internal Whse Processes  Information System  Warehouse  Physical Inventory  With Bin Inventory Management   Evaluation of Quant Inventory
Transaction Code LI15

Note- In a stock movement, if you move a quant from one storage bin to another, the system deletes the quant in the source storage bin and creates a new quant in the destination storage bin. Because the system stores the inventory data for the cycle counting inventory at quant level in the quant data, any inventory data that you previously entered is lost in this type of stock transfer.

Conclusion- This method is very useful. we dont want to block all stock or bin for a particular material so we can use it by blocking a quant. It will help us to run business smoothly without impacting.

Please share your feedback to improve it 

References- https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/b2dee5e83e2446149294f9860a7c08f0/278ec95360267214e10000000a174cb4.html

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/cycle-counting-physical-inventory-in-sap-wm-at-bin-level/ba-p/13938236 

 

Thanks

entry.title: Concept for Warehouse Billing in EWM
entry.content:
Hi All

I am going to explain warehouse billing Concept in EWM.

Introduction- In EWM, we can record the quantity of warehouse services used for the services agreed upon in an agreement in the Transportation Management. We can subsequently send this quantity information back to the TM for charge calculation and settlement based on the charges you have agreed to in the agreement

Benefits-

1- Sell warehouse services to customers and bill the customers periodically based the services used for a time period

2- Purchase warehouse services from external service providers and periodically pay the service provider based on the warehouse services used for a time period.

Available Features in Warehouse Billing

1- Snapshot management
2- Warehouse billing measurement request management
3- Warehouse billing measurement management

We will describe each of one 

1- Snapshot Management - We use this feature to capture the EWM business data relevant to measuring warehouse services and for calculating the charges for billing

We can record below mentioned business data in snapshot management

1- Snapshot of Physical Stock - We create a snapshot for stock at a particular time. The system records the warehouse inventory data at this particular time. We can create snapshots to record the presence of physical stock on a daily basis. If We create multiple snapshots in one day, the system merges the physical stock on that specific date.

2- Snapshot for Inbound Deliveries - The system records business data for goods that have been received and confirmed in Inbound Delivery Document (Document Type PDI). If the inbound delivery items are partially received, the item is recorded partially with the quantity

3- Snapshot for Outbound Deliveries-The system records business data for goods that have been sent and have a completed status in Outbound Delivery Document (Document Type FDO).

4- Snapshot for Warehouse tasks-The system records data for warehouse tasks that have been carried out and confirmed

We below Scenarios to perform 

 1- We can Create Snapshots to create snapshots on a daily basis
2- Configure Snapshots to activate/deactivate, and specify other settings for the snapshots
3- To create snapshots using specify attributes, we can create snapshots for Physical stock, Inbound               deliveries, Outbound deliveries and warehouse tasks.

We can delete and remove snapshots

2- Request for Warehouse Billing Measurement- EWM automatically creates a request for warehouse billing measurement (WBMR) from the Transportation Management (TM) agreement data. In the WBMR, we can assign warehouse billing measurement services to measure the warehouse services used, and periodically create warehouse billing measurements (WBMs) for calculating the quantity of services used for a billing period. The system uses the WBM information for calculating charges for the warehouse services used in the TM. When agreement information in TM is changed, the WBMR reflects the changes in the EWM.

WBMR Status

In Process
Activated
Deactivated

When you save and release an agreement in TM, EWM automatically receives the agreement as a request for warehouse billing measurement (WBMR). The WBMR has the same billing schedule determined in the agreement in TM. The billing schedule refers to a specified factory calendar both in TM and EWM. You must specify the same factory calendar settings in both the systems.

Warehouse Billing Measurement Service Assignment 

We can calculate the quantity of warehouse services used after you assign a warehouse billing measurement service to an item in a request for warehouse billing measurement document. We can measure the service quantity for the following EWM documents
Number of documents

Product quantity

Weight

Volume

Number of bins

Number of handling units

We can also specify a variant along with the warehouse billing measurement service in the WBMR item to enter specific constraints to measure warehouse services. For example, we can constrain the measurement to a specific group of documents, products, bins, or handling unit.

Note-The system subsequently measures the quantity from EWM business data in snapshots

3- Warehouse Billing Measurement Management-

After we have activated a request for billing measurement, we can display the generated warehouse billing measurements (WBM) in the Warehouse Management Monitor under Billing Warehouse Billing Measurement. The WBM contains the quantity of warehouse services used. A WBM can be generated for every billing period we have defined. The quantity in the WBM is obtained from the business data recorded into the snapshots. In the Warehouse Management Monitor, you can see the business data that was used to calculate the quantity as the proof of services.

Billing measurement document has following statuses

Ready for Distribution
Distributed
No Distribution

Reporting for Warehouse Billing in EWM Warehouse Monitor

For reporing we can use warehouse management monitor-Billing-Snapshots
For reporting we can use warehouse management monitor - Billing - Request for Warehouse Billing Measurement.

For reporing we can use warehouse management monitor-Billing-Billing Measurement

 

Please share your feedback to improve it 

 

Reference-https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/9832125c23154a179bfa1784cdc9577a/5ad63855b04c6a38e10000000a174cb4.html

 

 

Regerads

Rashid

entry.title: Service with Advanced Execution: Process Flow 2 – Early Planning before Service Order Release
entry.content:
Service with Advanced Execution is a new service business process in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. This is based on the integration of Service with Maintenance Management.

The concept of Execution Status in Service with Advanced Execution is introduced in the following blog:

Execution Status in Service with Advanced Execution

Within Service with Advanced Execution, there are various process flows depending on the business scenario. These process flows are distinguished based on two aspects:

  • How the process starts, and which is the first transaction.
  • What is the trigger for the creation of the maintenance order and eventually the start of planning.

Process Flow 2:

This blog showcases the process flow wherein the initial transaction is the service order, and the maintenance order is created before the release of the execution order item in the service order. This means the maintenance order is created and planning is started before the commercial aspects were finalized by the Service Manager.

This approach is especially beneficial when a lead time is required before starting the service execution. For example, if a spare part is required to start the service and it takes 10 days to order and procure it. The maintenance planner can proactively initiate the procurement process a couple of weeks before the scheduled start of the job. This way, the necessary components are available on time, avoiding delays and ensuring the service can be performed as per the planned schedule.

To enable early creation of a billable maintenance order, the following conditions must be met:

  • The execution order item must be in the In Process status.
  • The execution order item does not have any associated billable maintenance order.
  • There are no errors on the execution order item.

Creation of Service Order

A service order can be created using the ‘Create Service Order’ App.

The following details could be filled while creating a service order document:

  • Sold-to-Party
  • Organizational data such as Sales Organization and optionally Service Organization
  • Parties involved such as Service Employee Group and Employee Responsible
  • Execution Order Item with Planning Plant, Work Center, DIP Profile and Billing Relevance
  • Reference Objects

After entering all the details, save the service order document. The service order gets created with an execution order item with lifecycle status as ‘Open’. The lifecycle status of the execution order item can be changed to ‘In Process’ while the scope of the service and other details of the item are being negotiated between the Service Manager and the customer.

In the Items assignment block, the execution order items can be filtered and the Send to Planning column will be displayed. To create the billable maintenance order, the Send to Planning checkbox can be marked for the execution order item in ‘In Process’ status. The billable maintenance order is created in CRTD status. The execution status on the execution order item is set as ‘Execution Planning’ as soon as the maintenance order is created.

The document flow between the service order and maintenance order can be viewed in Transaction History assignment block.

The details of the maintenance order connected to the execution order item can be viewed in the Maintenance Order assignment block in the item details page.

Status lifecycle in Service with Advanced Execution

Current state of an execution order item

The state diagram illustrates the status lifecycle of one execution order item along with its corresponding maintenance order in this process flow. Each block below represents a state displaying 3 different status values. The lifecycle and execution status of an execution order item along with the status of the corresponding maintenance order.

Note: The execution order item can be rejected only when the execution status is ‘Execution Planning’.

Release of Maintenance Order

Once the maintenance order is created from the service order, the maintenance order is in planning phase. After the planning is done and once the spare parts are available, the maintenance order is set to ‘REL’ (Released) by the Maintenance Planner. Subsequently, the execution order item is also set to ‘Released’ automatically and the execution of service can be started. At this stage, the execution status on the execution order item is set to ‘In Execution’.

Completion of Execution Order Item

After the execution of service is completed by the Maintenance Technician, the maintenance order status is set to ‘TECO’ (Technically Completed).  With this status transition, the lifecycle status of execution order item is set to ‘Completed’ automatically. The execution status on the execution order item is set to ‘Execution Done’.

Summary

The distinct feature in this process flow is the creation of maintenance order before the release of the execution order item in the service order. This helps the maintenance planner to start early planning to accommodate a lead time before the start of execution. As we proceed with the execution of the job, the lifecycle and execution status of execution order item are in continuous alignment with the status of maintenance order at every step.

 

Further references

To know more about the execution order item and its attributes, please refer following blog:

Deep Dive: Execution Order Item and Billable Maintenance Order

entry.title: Exploit SAP Standard Functionalties to handle Returnable Transport Packaging Processes
entry.content:
1. Introduction

Returnable Transport Packaging is the process that deals with the packaging return from your company to the vendor once the delivery has been fully received at your premise. These kind of goods are legally owned by your vendor and are managed in SAP using the special stock type “M - Ret.trans.pkg vendor”.    In this article, I will use GAS as roh material and tank cylinder and RTP material.

The main characteristics of purchasing cycle for these materials are the following:

  • Material master we need to select the material type as RTP material with type “LEIH”
  • Creation of purchase requisition and purchase order, for roh materials, is following the usual procedure based on company standards
  • The goods receipt are posted with MvT 101 for roh materials and 501 M for RTP materials.
  • Stock level is tracking using special stock “M”
  • Returns are managed via MvT 502 M

The main business activities to be performed in the SAP are reported in the Figure-1:

Figure 1 – Main activities involved in the Returnable Transport Packaging Process

2. System Activities

The business activities that will be shown in the system are the following:

  • Master Data Management
    • Create Material Master (ROH/ LEIH)
    • Create Vendor Master (BP)
    • Create Purchasing Info Record
    • Maintain Condition Record
    • Create G/L Accounts
  • Transactional Data
    • Create Purchase Order
    • Post Goods Receipt (GR) & add RTP material (via MvT 501 M)
    • Post the vendor invoice (LIV)
    • Register the payment
    • Return RTP to the vendor (via MvT 502 M)
  • Reports
    • Display the logistic/financial documents associated with the GR
    • Display the logistic/financial documents associated with the LIV
    • Display the RTP stock level
    • Display the financial vendor’s open items
    • Finance (list of accounting documents posted for Vendor’s Account)
  • IMG settings
    • Logistics (Material type settings, GR/IR Condition Records)
    • Finance (Automatic Account Determination settings)
    • Accounts Payable (LIV settings)

Let us delve deeper into these steps in SAP now.

2.1 Master Data Management 

2.1.1 Material Master Data

The master data management team will create returnable packaging materials via transaction MM01 or following the menu path: Logistics > Materials Management > Material Master > Material > Create (General) > Immediately. This kind of materials are defined “pipeline” materials and are represented in SAP by the material type “LEIH”, as shown in the Figure-2.        

Figure 2 – Create Returnable Packaging Material (Material Type)

Select the appropriate views, as shown in the Figure-3.

 

Figure 3 – Create Returnable Packaging Material (Views)

In the Figure-4, you can find the mandatory data to be maintained for the Basis data 1 (Base Unit of Measure and material Group).

Figure 4 – Create Returnable Packaging Material (Basic data 1 View)

In the purchasing view, specify the buyer or group of buyers resposnible for such material, as shown in the Figure-5.

Figure 5 – Create Returnable Packaging Material (Purchasing View)

In the MRP 1 and MRP 3 Views, specify the MRP type and Availability Check as shown in the Figure-6 and Figure-7.

Figure 6 – Create Returnable Packaging Material (MRP 1 View)

Figure 7 – Create Returnable Packaging Material (MRP 3 View)

In the Accounting 1 view, specify the valuation class “3050”, price control, price unit, initial moving average price and standard price, as shown in the Figure-8.

Figure 8 – Create Returnable Packaging Material (Accounting 1 View)

Open the Accounting View 2 and confirm the pop-up message, shown the Figure-9:

Figure 9 – Create Returnable Packaging Material (Accounting 2 View)

At this stage, the system will give the material number, as shown the Figure-10:

Figure 10 – Create Returnable Packaging Material (Material Number)

The master data management team will create Roh materials via transaction MM01 or following the menu path: Logistics > Materials Management > Material Master > Material > Create (General) > Immediately.

This kind of materials are defined in SAP by the material type “ROH”, as shown in the Figure-11.        

Figure 11 – Create Roh Material (Material Type)

Then, select the appropriate views, as shown in the Figure-12.


Figure 12 – Create Roh Material (Material Views)

Then, select the organizational levels (e.g.: Plant and Storage Location) and fill the data per each selected view. In the Figure-13, you can find the mandatory data to be maintained for the Basis data 1 (e.g.: Base Unit of Measure and material Group).

Figure 13 – Create Roh Material (Basic Data 1)

In the purchasing view, specify the buyer or group of buyers resposnible for such material, activate the variable order unit, in our case 1 KG ~ 2l, as shown in the Figure-14.

Figure 14 – Create Roh Material (Purchasing View)

In the MRP 1 and MRP 3 Views, specify the MRP type and Availability Check as shown in the Figure-15 and Figure-16.

Figure 15 – Create Roh Material (MRP 1 View)

Figure 16 – Create Roh Material (MRP 3 View)

In the Figure-17, you can see the details of the Plant data/storage view 1.

Figure 17 – Create Roh Material (Plant data/Storage 1 View)

In the Accounting 1 view, specify the valuation class “3001”, price control, price unit, initial moving average price and standard price, as shown in the Figure-18.

Figure 18 – Create Roh Material (Accounting 1 View)

Open the Accounting View 2 and confirm the pop-up message, shown the Figure-19:

Figure 19 – Create Roh Material (Accounting 2 View)

At this stage, the system will give the material number, as shown the Figure-20:

Figure 20 – Create Roh Material (Material Number)

2.1.2 Create Vendor

The master data management team will create the vendor via transaction BP or following the menu path: Logistics > Settlement Management > Environment > Master Data > Maintain Business Partner. Specify as BP Role “00000” and enter the address of your BP and then save the data entered, the system will give the BP number, as shown the Figure-21:

Figure 21 – Create BP (General View)

Create the Purchasing view, choosing as BP role “FLVN01”, as shown the Figure-22:

Figure 22 – Change BP (BP Role “FLVN01”)

Enter the Purchasing details (e.g.: Partner Functions), as shown the Figure-23:

Figure 23 – Change BP (Purchasing View)

Saving the data, the system will give the confirmation message, as shown the Figure-24:

Figure 24 – Information Message (BP data updates)

Create the Company Code view, choosing as BP role “FLVN00”, as shown the Figure-25:

Figure 25 – Change BP (BP Role “FLVN00”)

Enter the Company Code details (e.g.: Reconciliation Account and sort key), as shown the Figure-26:

Figure 26 – Change BP (Company Code View)

Saving the data, the system will give the confirmation message, as shown the Figure-27:

Figure 27 – Information Message (BP data updates)

2.1.3 Create Purchasing Info Record

The purchasing department will  create the pipeline purchasing info record using the transaction ME11 or follow the menu path: Logistics > Materials Management > Purchasing > Master Data > Info Record > Create. The system will propose the screen as shown in the Figure-28.

Note that if the Plant is having more than one purchase organization then create info record with that plant standard purchase organization only and standard purchase organization info record price only will be relevant for automatic settlement.

Figure 28 – Create Standard Info Record (Initial Screen)

In the “General Data” screen, specify the supplier and Origin data as needed,  as shown in the Figure-29.

Figure 29 – Create Standard Info Record (General Data)

In the “Purchasing Organization data 1” screen, specify the control and conditions details as shown in the Figure-30. Note that, apart from the price details, you need to also enter the tax code otherwise you will get an error during the settlement posting. That’s why some Organization prefer to also have the tax code filed mandatory in the Purchasing Info Record.

Figure 30 – Create Standard Info Record (Purchasing Organization data 1)

Then, saving the data, the system will generate the Purchasing info record document number, as shown in the Figure-31:

Figure 31 – Create Standard Info Record (Document Number)

2.1.4 Maintain Condition Record

The condition records are prerequisites to generate the expected output to further process the Purchase Order (e.g.: printing, faxing, electronic transmission) to vendor. The purchasing condition record can be maintained using the transaction code MN04 or following the menu path: Logistics > Materials Management > Purchasing > Master Data > Messages > Purchase Order > Create. In the selection screen, enter the output type “NEU” and select the key combination, as shown in the Figure-32.

Figure 32 – Create Condition Record for Output Type NEU (Initial Screen)

In the next screen, enter the document type “NB” and go to the “Communication” option, as shown in the Figure-33.

Figure 33 – Create Condition Record for Output Type NEU (Line Item)

In the next screen, specify your device and other parameters as shown in the Figure-34.

Figure 34 – Create Condition Record for Output Type NEU (Communication Details)

After specifying the output details, upon saving the system will give the information message, as shown in the Figure-35.

 

Figure 35 – Create Condition Record for Output Type NEU (Confirmation Message)

Note that the maintenance of the logistics condition records are described in the Customizing Settings for Materials Management.

2.1.5 Create G/L Accounts

The finance department will create the G/L account either via transaction FS00 or following the menu path: Accounting > Financial Accounting > General Ledger > Master Records > G/L Accounts > Individual Processing > Centrally. In  our case, we will use the transaction FS00 to display the details of the G/L accounts involved in the pipeline process, as shown in the Figure-36, Figure-37, Figure-38, and Figure-39.

Figure 36 – Display G/L Account Centrally (Consumption Row Material)

Control Data Tab Data:

  • Account Currency: EUR
  • Balances in local currency only: marked
  • Tax Category: “-“ Only input tax allowed

Create/bank/interest Tab Data:

  • Field status group: G006 “Material accounts”
  • Post Automatic Only: Option flagged

Figure 37 – Display G/L Account Centrally (Accounts payable-domestic)

Control Data Tab Data:

  • Account Currency: EUR
  • Balances only in local currency: marked
  • Tax Category: “-“ Only input tax allowed
  • Sort Key: 014 - Purchase order

Create/bank/interest Tab Data:

  • Field status group: G045 “Goods/invoice received clearing accounts”
  • Post Automatic Only: Option flagged

Figure 38 – Display G/L Account Centrally (Payables from consignment warehouse)

Control Data Tab Data:

  • Account Currency: EUR
  • Tax Category: “-“ Only input tax allowed
  • Reconciliation Account for Acct Type: K - Vendors

Create/bank/interest Tab Data:

  • Field status group: G067 “Reconciliation accounts”
  • Post Automatically Only: Option flagged

Figure 39 – Display G/L Account Centrally (Input Tax)

Control Data Tab Data:

  • Account Currency: EUR
  • Balances in Local Currency Only: Option flagged
  • Tax Category: < Input Tax Account
  • Open Item Management: Option flagged

Create/bank/interest Tab Data:

  • Field status group: G014 “MM adjustment accounts”
  • Post Automatically Only: Option flagged

2.2 Transactional Data Management

2.2.1 Create Purchase Order

The procurement department will create the Purchase Order (PO) either using the transaction ME21N or following the menu path: Logistics > Materials Management > Purchasing > Purchase Order > Create > Vendor/Supplying Plant Known.

Then, specify the data as reported in the Figure-40.

Figure 40 – Create Purchase Order (Line Item data)

Make sure that the condition record has been determined to allow further PO processing (e.g.: PO printing), as reported in the Figure-41

Figure 41 – Create Goods Issue (Document Number)

After specifying the required data and upon saving, the system will give the Purchase Order  document number, as shown in the Figure-42.

Figure 42 – Create Purchase Order (Document Number)

2.2.2 Perform Goods Receipt

To post the goods receipt, the logistics team can either use the transaction MIGO or follow the menu path: Logistics > Materials Management > Inventory Management > Goods Movement > Goods Movement. Then, specify the data as reported in the Figure-43 and Figure-44. First, specify the appropriate purchase order document number and after specifying the receving data for the roh (e.g.: qty, storage location), then, specify the details of the transport Packaging, pressing the option transport equipment "". At this stage, the system will propose a new line item to specify the data related to the transport material and in particular the MvT 501 M “Receipt to RTP”.

Figure 43 – Post GR Data Entry (I)

In the following screen, you can see the MvT associated to both line items:

Figure 44 – Post GR Data Entry (II)

Then, check your document and post it. The system will generate the material document number, as shown in the Figure-45.

Figure 45 – Post GR Data Entry (Material document number)

To display the GR material and financial documents, you can use the transaction MIGO, enter the document number stated in the Figure-45 and then, press the option “FI Documents”, as reported in the Figure-46.

Figure 46 – Display GR Material Document

Then, pressing the option “FI Documents” and selecting the accounting documents, the system will show the FI/ML documents, as reported in the Figure-47 and Figure-48.

Figure 47 – Display GR Finance Document

 

Figure 48 – Display GR Material Ledger Document

2.2.3 Check Stock Level

To check the stock level, you can use the transaction MMBE follow the manu path: Logistics > Materials Management > Inventory Management > Environment > Stock > Stock Overview. Then, specify the RTP material and plant, storage location in the selection screen and run the report. The system will show the details as reported in the Figure-49.

Figure 49 – Display RTP Stock Level

At this stage, if you double-click on the RT Packaging level, the system will give you the details of the stock belonging to your vendor, as shown in the Figure-50.

Figure 50 – Display RTP Stock Level & Vendor Info

In the below Figure-51, you can find the stock level of the roh material.

Figure 51 – Display Roh Stock Level

2.2.4 Post Vendor Invoice

To post the vendor invoice, the Account Payable department will use either the transaction MIRO or follow the menu path: Logistics > Materials Management > Logistics Invoice Verification > Document Entry > Enter Invoice. In the Figure-52 and Figure-53, you can see respectively the main invoice data and the simulation of the invoice posting.

Figure 52 – LIV Data Entry (Basic Data)

Figure 53 – LIV Data Entry (posting simulation)

After checking that the balance of the document is “0”, you can post "" the document.

The system will give you the LIV document number, as shown on the Figure-54.

Figure 54 – LIV Data Entry (Document Number)

2.2.5 Display Invoice Document

You can display the invoice created in the previous step via transaction MIR4 or following the menu path: Logistics > Materials Management > Logistics Invoice Verification > Further Processing > Display Invoice Document. Then, enter the document number, FY and choose the option “Display Document”, as shown in the Figure-55.

Figure 55 – Display LIV Document (Selection Screen)

The system will show the details as reported in the Figure-56.

Figure 56 – Display LIV Document (Material Document)

Then, choose the option “Follow-On Documents”, the system will display the details of the FI document, as shown in the Figure-57.

Figure 57 – Display LIV Document (Finance Document)

2.2.6 Payment Run

  • The payment can be done either via regular process or via credit note (if legally admitted)
  • This process is planned by the account payable department and executed by treasury
  • The standard transactions to perform  the payments are the followig
    • F110 “Parameters for Automatic Payment” also reached via menu path: Accounting > Financial Accounting > Accounts Receivable > Periodic Processing > Payments. This transaction automates the payment process by selecting vendor invoices due for payment, generating payment files and post the payment documents on the system, widely used for regular payments run.
    • F-53 “Post Outgoing Payments”, manually triggered, also reached via menu path: Accounting > Financial Accounting > Accounts Payable > Document Entry > Outgoing Payment > Post. This transaction is used to post outgoing payments, such as vendor payments. It updates the accounts payable and general ledger accordingly.
    • F-58 “Payment with Printout”, manually triggered, also reached via manu path: Accounting > Financial Accounting > Accounts Payable > Document Entry > Outgoing Payment > Post + Print Forms. This transaction is used to execute payment run, which generate payment documents for multiple vendor invoices based on defined selection criteria.

2.2.7 Return RTP Materials

To post the return of the RTP materials, the logistics team can either use the transaction MIGO or follow the menu path: Logistics > Materials Management > Inventory Management > Goods Movement > Goods Movement. Then enter the following data, as reported in the Figure-58:

  1. A01 Goods receipt & R10 Other
  2. MvT 502 M
  3. RTP material number & Qty to be returned
  4. Vendor owned the RTP materials

Figure 58 – Return RTP Materials (Data Entry).

Then checking the document, the system will give the info message as shown in the Figure-59:

Figure 59 – Return RTP Materials (Check results)

Then posting the document, the system will give the material document number, as shown in the Figure-60:

Figure 60 – Return RTP Materials (Document number)

To display the material document associated to the return and verify that no financial documents have been posted, you can use the transaction MIGO, enter the document number stated in the Figure-61 and then, press the option “FI Documents”, as reported in the Figure-61.

Figure 61 – Display RTP Material Document 

To process the output type of this document and to print it out, you can use the transaction MB90 or follow the menu path: Logistics > Materials Management > Inventory Management > Material Document > Process output. In the selection screen of the report, enter the data as specify in the Figure-62.

Figure 62 – Process Output Type for RTP Material Document (Selection Screen)

Executing the report, the following details will be provided:

Figure 63 – Process Output Type for RTP Material Document (Output Screen)

Select the record and process it.

The following details will be provided:

Figure 64 – Process Output RTP Material Document (Confirmation message)

Important Note

If you will get an ABAP Runtime Error upon the printing preview, via MB90 or SP01, due to this issue: Exception condition "FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED" triggered, then you can refer to the OSS Note# https://me.sap.com/notes/0003376098.

2.3 Reports

In the following paragraph you can find the main standard reports available in the system to monitor this flow from logistics and financial points of view. In the Figure-65 and Figure-66, you can find the selection and output screens of the “Material Document List” report, reached via transaction MB51.

Figure 65 – Material Document List (Selection Screen)

Figure 66 – Material Document List (Output Screen)

In the Figure-67 and Figure-68, you can find the selection and output screens of the “Vendor Line Item Display” report, reached via transaction FBL1N. This transaction is used to display the line-items details for a specific vendor, like information on invoices, payments, and open items with associated due dates to be processed.

Figure 67 – Vendor Line Item Display (Selection Screen)

Executing the report, the system will show the following details:

Figure 68 – Vendor Line Item Display (Output Screen)

2.4 Customizing Settings

In the following paragraphs I am going to report only the most important customizing settings to be checked in the MM and FI modules.

2.4.1 Materials Management relevant customizing

a. Verify the Material Type used for RTP

To check the attribute of the material type “LEIH”, you can use the IMG transaction OMS2 and Select material type LEIH. Then, the system will report the details shown in the Figure-69.

Figure 69 – Material Type RTP (Details)

In the Figure-70, you can see the details of the quantity/value updating.

Figure 70 – Material Type PLEIH (Quantity/Value updating overview)

b. Create the condition records for the GR/GI documents.

To create the condition records for GR/GI documents, you can either use the IMG transaction MN21 or follow the menu path: SPRO > Materials Management > Inventory Management and Physical Inventory > Output Determination > Maintain Conditions > Create Condition: Inventory Mgmt.

  • For GR, you can use the Output Type “WE03” and perform actions as described in the Figure-71, 72, 73, 74.

Figure 71 – Define GR Condition Record (Selection Screen)

 

Figure 72 – Define GR Condition Record (Details)

Figure 73 – Define GR Condition Record (Communication)

Then, save your record:

Figure 74 – Define GR Condition Record (Saving Confirmation)

  • For GI, you can use the output type “WA03” and perform actions as described in the Figure-75, 76, 77, 78.

Figure 75 – Define GI Condition Record (Selection Screen)

Figure 76 – Define GI Condition Record (Details)

Figure 77 – Define GI Condition Record (Communication)

 

Figure 78 – Define GI Condition Record (Saving Confirmation)

 

2.4.2 Financial Accounting Settings

a. Check the settings done for the definition of the Tax Code “A9” via transaction FTXP.

Figure 79 – Display Tax Code A9 (Tax percentage rate)

Then, selecting the option “Tax Accounts”, you will be able to display the G/L Account associated with this tax code, as shown in the Figure-80.

Figure 80 – Display Tax Code A9 (G/L Assignment)

a. Settings for the Automatic Account Determination via IMG transaction OBYC.

The following accounts are updated during MIGO (MvT 101):

  • Debit the stock account “300010 - Row material 2” via BSX entry
  • Credit the GR/IR account “191100” via WRX entry

The following accounts are updated during MIRO:

  • Debit the GR/IR account “191100” via WRX entry
  • Credit the Vendor account “160000” via Vendor# 100006
  • Credit the input tax account “154000” via VST entry

In the Figure-81, you can find the configuration account defined for the transaction “BSX”.

Figure 81 – Configuration Accounting maintain for transaction “BSX”

In the Figure-82, you can find the configuration account defined for the transaction “WRX”.

Figure 82 – Configuration Accounting maintain for transaction “WRX”

In the Figure-83, you can find the configuration account defined for the transaction “VST”.

Figure 83 – Configuration Accounting maintain for transaction “VST”

2.4.3 Accounts Payable Settings

a. Define Tolerance Groups for Employees

In this activity you specify the various amount limits for your employees (e.g.: posting invoice, payments, grant discounted…). You can also define tolerances without specifying tolerance groups. In  this case leave the field Grp blank. The tolerances then apply to all  employees that have not been assigned to a group. Financial Accounting > Accounts Receivable and Accounts Payable > Business Transactions > Open Item Clearing > Clearing Differences > Define Tolerance Groups for Employees. Figure-84.

Figure 84 – Define FI Tolerance Groups for Users

HINT: In the previous IMG transaction, there must be at least one entry for each company code, otherwise the user will not be able to post the LIV via MIRO and will get the following error message: "No amount authorization for customers/vendors in company code Z001" . Message No. F5155

Diagnosis: No amount authorization for customer/vendor line items has been specified in company code Z001 for the user group to which you are assigned. Provided that you are not explicitly assigned to a user group, the amount authorization to the group blank (" ") is valid.

Procedure: If you entered the correct company code, initiate the maintenance of tables T043 (user groups) and/or T043T (company code authorizations).

3. Conclusion

Following this article, the readers will be able to check the main IMG settings in system, master data and record the transactional data needed to manage the Returnable Transport Packaging processes. Additionally, this can be used as guideline, across project phases, for anybody involved in SAP Logistic activities, despite the release of the system.

entry.title: S/4 HANA Extensibility – Guide to extend API_BUSINESS_PARTNER API for Standard Fields
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community Members,

We hope this blog finds you well.

In this, I will explain how to identify and extend API_BUSINESS_PARTNER API for Standard fields.

Business Scenario:

API_BUSINESS_PARTNER is a standard API for creating/updating the Business Partner in S/4 HANA.

But few standard Business Partner fields are missing as part of the API.

In this blog, will extend KNB1-LOCKB (Lock Box) field. Similarly other standard fields can be extended.

Finding the Service Definition for the Standard API

The first step is to locate the CDS Data Definitions. For our example, the CDS Data Definition is A_BusinessPartner. You can find the CDS Data Definitions using the sql view name or alternatively, you can use Eclipse to perform a pattern search with the CDS Data Definition name.

Once you have the CDS Data Definition, identify the fields within the entities present in the data definition. For our requirement, we needed to add the lockb field. The path for this field is as follows:

  • A_BusinessPartner
    •  A_Customer
      • A_CustomerCompany

Creating Extend View

Now extend the CDS view (A_CustomerCompany) using Eclipse. This is done by creating a new “Data Definition” element and the “Extend View” template.

  1.  Launch Eclipse and connect to your system.
  2.  Click File -> New -> Other -> Data Definition
  3.  Enter Name and Description, click next and select transport.
  4.  Select ‘Extend View’ Template.
  5.  Add the additional fields lockb from another I_CustomerCompany CDS view.
  6.  Activate your extension

Important Notes:

Make sure view field name(lockb) is same as table field name(knb1-lockb). So that the data mapping is done automatically.

Conclusion

You have successfully extended the standard API. The newly added field can now be seen in the standard API response.

entry.title: Resolving the "Enqueue Operation Without Progress" Error in SAP & Avoiding SPOF
entry.content:
SAP Issue: Enqueue Operation Without Progress Due to Max Connection Limit Breach

Introduction

In complex SAP environments, maintaining smooth communication between various components like the SAP Dispatcher, Message Server, and Enqueue Replicator is crucial for system stability. A frequent issue that arises in such scenarios is the "Enqueue Operation Without Progress" error. This typically occurs when the SAP Dispatcher loses connection to the Message Server because of a breached maximum connection limit. In this blog, we will explore this issue in detail, identify the root cause, and provide solutions to prevent such problems from occurring.

Symptoms: How to Recognize the Problem

If your SAP system is experiencing the Enqueue Operation Without Progress issue, you will notice the following errors in the Dispatcher logs:



Sat May 11 20:05:30:581 2023 *** WARNING => DpWpOperationWithoutProgress: found W303 with enqueue operation running >= 473 sec [dpxxdisp.c 4660]

 

Sat May 11 20:06:01:599 2023 DpUpdateStatusFileWith: state=YELLOW, reason=Enqueue operation without progress

 

Sat May 11 20:06:30:612 2023 *** WARNING => DpWpOperationWithoutProgress: found W294 with enqueue operation running >= 37 sec [dpxxdisp.c 4660]

 

Sat May 11 20:07:01:628 2023 DpUpdateStatusFileWith: state=YELLOW, reason=Enqueue operation without progress

 

Sat May 11 20:07:30:644 2023 *** WARNING => DpWpOperationWithoutProgress: found W294 with enqueue operation running >= 97 sec [dpxxdisp.c 4660]

 

Sat May 11 20:08:01:662 2023 DpUpdateStatusFileWith: state=YELLOW, reason=Enqueue operation without progress

 

In these logs, the recurring "Enqueue operation without progress" warning appears, indicating that the dispatcher is unable to proceed with enqueue operations, usually because of a connection issue with the Message Server. The log entries show that certain enqueue operations (such as W191 and W4) are running for extended periods, which is unusual and points to a potential network bottleneck or configuration issue.

Root Cause: Max Connection Limit Breach

The problem is often caused by the SAP system reaching its maximum connection limit, which leads to a situation where the Dispatcher cannot connect to the Message Server. This is commonly triggered by an overload of connections managed by the Internet Communication Manager (ICM) or the Enqueue Replicator.

As indicated in the logs, the system can reach its ICM connection limit, which means that the dispatcher or enqueue replicator is unable to accept new connections due to the maximum allowed connections being reached. In the log, you will see entries like:

less

 

[2023-11-03-14:20:31,983] [Thr 140356127720704] *** WARNING => IcmAcceptClient: server overload: conn: 4000 - accept delayed [icxxman.c 5779]

[2023-11-03-14:20:31,984] [Thr 140356127720704] Trigger snapshot

[2023-11-03-14:20:31,984] [Thr 140354322437888] Client IP table - not enabled or not yet initialized

 

Additionally, a Server Snapshot will indicate that the issue stems from reaching the maximum number of ICM connections, which results in delays in processing requests.

Key Configuration Parameters to Adjust

To resolve the issue and avoid it in the future, certain SAP parameters related to connections need to be optimized. These key parameters should be reviewed and adjusted:

1. enq/io/max_conn

  • Description: This parameter controls the maximum number of connections allowed for enqueue operations.

Recommendation: Ensure that enq/io/max_conn is set higher than the total number of work processes across all application servers.
Example: If you have 4 application servers and each server has 100 work processes, the total number of work processes will be 400. Therefore, enq/io/max_conn should be set to a value higher than this.


enq/io/max_conn = 10000

 

2. enq/io/max_threads

  • Description: This parameter specifies the maximum number of threads for processing connections at the enqueue server level. It controls the maximum number of threads the system can use to process incoming requests.

Recommendation: It is advisable not to set enq/io/max_threads higher than 4-6 threads based on best practices.


enq/io/max_threads = 4

  • According to SAP documentation (https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/si/3362959230.html), setting enq/io/max_threads higher than 4-6 is not recommended.

3. icm/max_conn

  • Description: This parameter specifies the maximum number of connections that the Internet Communication Manager (ICM) can handle.

Recommendation: Set icm/max_conn to a value greater than enq/io/max_conn, ideally 10,000, as a general rule of thumb.
Example:


icm/max_conn = 10000

  • This setting ensures that the ICM can handle all incoming connections without reaching the maximum limit.

Solution Approach

To resolve the issue, follow these steps:

  1. Check the current settings for the key parameters (enq/io/max_conn, icm/max_conn, and enq/io/max_threads) in your system and adjust them based on the recommendations above.
  2. Ensure that the ICM connection limit (icm/max_conn) is greater than the enq/io/max_conn value to ensure proper handling of incoming connections.
  3. Ensure the enq/io/max_threads value is set to an optimal number (e.g., 4-6 threads) to avoid overloading the Enqueue Replicator.
  4. Monitor the system after applying the changes to verify that the issue has been resolved. Use tools like ST03N or ST11 to monitor the connection behavior and ensure that the connection pool is not being exhausted.

Additional Considerations

  • Network Resources: Ensure that network resources such as bandwidth, latency, and server CPU are adequate to handle the number of connections configured. Overloading the system with connections can still lead to performance degradation even if the parameters are optimized.
  • SAP Notes and Documentation: Always refer to the latest SAP Notes and documentation for updates on configuration best practices. For example, SAP Note 2946473 provides insights into ICM connection management.

Conclusion

The Enqueue Operation Without Progress issue is typically a result of reaching the maximum connection limit in the SAP environment. By adjusting key configuration parameters like enq/io/max_conn, icm/max_conn, and enq/io/max_threads, you can resolve this issue and ensure smoother communication between SAP components. Additionally, always ensure that your network and server resources are adequate to support the number of concurrent connections required by your system.

For more detailed information and troubleshooting, check SAP's official documentation and the latest SAP Notes.

References

  • SAP Note 2946473: ICM and Enqueue Parameters
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/si/3362959230.html?q=enq/io/max_threads icm/max_conn
  • https://me.sap.com/notes/2288909/E - IcmAcceptClient: server overload
  • https://me.sap.com/notes/3196926/E -Enqueue Replicator 2 is in YELLOW status OR Enqueue Server 2 is in hung state

entry.title: How to use Derived RAP Business Events and enhance the standard SAP payload and add custom Filters
entry.content:
Introduction: In this post, we will discuss how to use Derived RAP Business Events and extend the payload that is sent to SAP Event Mesh in Event Based Architecture. We will also learn how to apply a custom Filter on the Business Event Topic with a real Business example. For starters, SAP Event Mesh is SAP’s event infrastructure solution and is a fully managed cloud service that allows applications to communicate through events.

 

What is a Derived Business Event?

Developing a RAP Business Event involves the definition of its payload and its implementation. In case you want to use an already implemented event with a different payload, you can make use of derived events. A derived event is defined with reference to an already implemented event and allows the definition of a payload that deviates from the one of the referenced events. Refer following help link to understand in detail Derived Business Events

 

Prerequisites for derived events in extensions:

If the derived event is defined in a behavior definition extension, the following prerequisites apply for the original business object:

  • The business object containing the event that is referenced by the derived event needs to be extensibility-enabled as described in RAP Extensibility-Enablement.
  • The event referenced by the derived event needs to be exposed in the C0 and C1 released RAP BO interface. If the referenced event has been added in a behavior extension, it is implicitly exposed in the RAP BO interface.
  • The CDS view entity which serves as the data source for the payload of the derived event needs to be C1 released.

 

Business scenario(example):

When a Maintenance Order is created or its status is changed in SAP S4 HANA system (using Tcode IW31 / IW32), a notification event is sent to Event Mesh with payload having only two fields (MaintenanceOrder and MaintenanceOrderType) as part of standard SAP design. Requirement here is to enhance/customize this payload and add additional fields related to Maintenance Order. Additionally add a custom Filter on the Business Event Topic to make sure only specific Order Types are sent to Event Mesh.

 

How was it solved (Step by step procedure):

Step1: First let us do the required configuration to send standard SAP notification Event for Maintenance Order. Maintenance Order Standard Business Event can be found on following Business Accelerator Hub link Maintenance Order Events. Notice the sample payload shown which has only two fields (MaintenanceOrder and MaintenanceOrderType)

 

Go to Tcode-“/IWXBE/OUTBOUND_CFG” and add the following Topic “/sap/s4/beh/maintenanceorder/v1/MaintenanceOrder/SetToInPlanning/v1” in the Outbound Binding Configuration for the Active channel which Basis Team already configured separately. The steps to create a channel is not covered in this post which is done typically by an SAP BASIS consultant. 

 

To apply filters to a Topic, we would click on filter button shown in above screenshot which takes us to below screen. But here it does not show any option to filter based on ‘Maintenance Order type’ field which we want to achieve as part of business requirement. From Step3 onwards we will see how to achieve this using Derived RAP Business Event.

 

Step2: Now that Outbound Binding Configuration is done, let us test it by creating a Maintenance Order using Tcode IW31 and see the Payload generated.

 

 

Once Maintenance Order is created as shown above, go to Tcode- “/IWXBE/EVENT_MONITOR”, choose Outbound Events radio button and provide the channel name and execute. We can see the Payload is generated by standard SAP and with only two fields.

 

Step3: Now from this step onwards we will use Derived RAP Business Events and extend the Payload with additional fields and add a custom filter.

Developing a derived Business event comprises the definition of a CDS view for the event payload and the definition of the derived event itself in a behavior definition or in a behavior definition extension.

Step3a: Create a data definition for a CDS view entity called ZMAINT_ORDER_DATASOURCE that selects from standard CDS view I_MaintenanceOrder. Add the key field “MaintenanceOrder” and add other required additional fields that we want to see in the Enhanced Payload. Add the following CDS Annotation “@Event.context.attribute: 'xsapordtype'” for the MaintenanceOrderType field, this will enable us to use this field as a Filter on the Business Event Topic in Tcode - /IWXBE/EVENT_MONITOR and /IWXBE/EVENT_FILTER.

The below created CDS view entity will provide the payload for the derived event.

@EndUserText.label: 'Test Managed / Derived Event'

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED

define view entity ZMAINT_ORDER_DATASOURCE

  as select from I_MaintenanceOrder

  {

  key I_MaintenanceOrder.MaintenanceOrder,

@event.context.attribute: 'xsapordtype'

      I_MaintenanceOrder.MaintenanceOrderType,

      I_MaintenanceOrder.MaintenanceOrderDesc,

      I_MaintenanceOrder.MaintenancePlant,

      I_MaintenanceOrder.MaintenanceNotification     

} 

Step3b: Create a Behavior Extension for the standard Behavior Definition- R_MaintenanceOrderTP as shown below. For quick reference pasted below is the standard Behavior Definition in which standard Business Events are defined.

 

The resulting BDEF extension looks like this below. Right click on class zbp_r_maintenanceordertp_be and create the class.

extension implementation in class zbp_r_maintenanceordertp_be unique;

 

Add the keywords extend behavior for Order { }. The derived event can now be defined within the curly brackets.

extension implementation in class zbp_r_maintenanceordertp_be unique;

extend behavior for Order

{ 

}

Define a derived event with the name ZSetToInPlanning and with reference to the existing event SetToInPlanning. Above created CDS view entity ZMAINT_ORDER_DATASOURCE need to be passed as parameter; which will serve as the payload for the derived event.

extension implementation in class zbp_r_maintenanceordertp_be unique;

extend behavior for Order

{

   managed event ZSetToInPlanning on SetToInPlanning parameter ZMAINT_ORDER_DATASOURCE;  

  }

 

Step3c: Create an new Z-Event Binding and provide any custom Type Namespace , SAP Object Type, Operation. And then click on ADD button and provide Entity Name and Entity Event Name. Here in the Entity Event Name we need to make sure to provide the Managed/Derived Event Name(ZSETTOINPLANNING) that was declared in the Behavior Extension.

 

Step3d: Next go to Tcode-“/IWXBE/OUTBOUND_CFG” and add the following Topic “ZXXX/s4/beh/MaintenanceOrder/SetToInPlanning/v1” in the Outbound Binding Configuration for the Active channel which Basis Team already configured separately.

 

Step3e: Next click on the Filter button in above screenshot or we can go to Tcode - /IWXBE/EVENT_FILTER directly, and we see below screen. When we click on + sign button, then the newly added custom Filter appears in the list. This appears because of the CDS annotation (“@Event.context.attribute: 'xsapordtype'”) that was added above in Step3a.

We can add the custom filter now based on Maintenance Order Type as shown below. As per this Filter, it will send only Order type ‘ZABC’ related events to Event Mesh as per Business requirement mentioned in the Business Scenario section above.

 

Step4: Now that all the development and configuration steps related to Derived Business Events is completed, let us test and verify the new extended Payload, by creating a new Maintenance Order of Order type ‘ZABC’, using Tcode – IW31 as shown below.

In the below generated payload, please notice the four new additional fields related to Maintenance Order (MaintenanceOrderDesc, MaintenancePlant, MaintenanceNotification, MaintenanceNotificationText)

 

Conclusion: Using the Derived Business Events, we were able to ADD custom Filters on the Business Event Topic and we also enhanced and customized the Payload to send additional fields as per Business requirement.

entry.title: S/4HANA Analytics: From Fiori app to a CDS view
entry.content:
S/4HANA Analytics: From Fiori app to a CDS view

 

If you work with S/4HANA Embedded Analytics as I do, you might share the impression that analyzing or researching the system can sometimes feel more time-consuming than coding or configuring reports yourself. In this post, I outline how to trace your path from an Analytical Fiori App to a CDS (Core Data Services) view—and beyond.

 

Why Should You Care?

The primary reason to understand this process is the reusability of built-in content. This can be beneficial for:

  • Extracting data for BW, SAP Datasphere, or other platforms.
  • Using the same CDS view in external BI tools like Analysis for Office or SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC).

 

The Process Explained

 

Start with the Fiori Apps Library Fiori Apps Library

 

I typically work in on-premise environments, so my first step is to search within the Fiori Apps Library with filters set for S/4HANA on-premise and ‘Analytical’ application types.

Begin your search on Overview pages, but feel free to tailor your search to your needs.

 

Once you identify a relevant app, there are generally three places where you can find the source CDS view:

  • The Library documentation itself.
  • A linked Help page.
  • The App Configuration.

In the documentation itself

 

This is the most straightforward scenario. If you find a promising app, switch to the Implementation Information tab, expand Configuration, and look for OData services with the _CDS suffix. Copy the name without the _CDS suffix, and you have your view.

 

Let us take an example of a Procurement Overview Page, here we can see the 3 CDS views listed for us:

 

In the Help page linked with documentation

 

If the documentation doesn’t specify a CDS view, return to the Product Features tab and locate any linked Help page.

 

An example of Supply Demand Overview - Detailed Analysis shows us just that.

 

On the Help page, you may find a list of relevant CDS views to explore.

 

 

 

Through App Configuration

 

If neither the Fiori Apps Library nor the Help page provides clear clues, but the app in question is an Analytical WebDynpro application, there’s still hope.

 

You can either find your CDS view further down in the Configuration or by launching a Fiori App in your sandbox environment.

 

Let us look at the example of Material Inventory Values - Balance Summary – as you can see, there is no specific OData service mentioned in the documentation.

Launch the Fiori app in your sandbox environment using the URL format: _host_/sap/bc/webdynpro/sap/_WD_application_

For the Material Inventory Values - Balance Summary app, use a URL like:

host:50080/sap/bc/webdynpro/sap/fis_fpm_ovp_ml_balsum

 

If this is a Web-Dynpro-based grid (very frequently the case), look for the Query Information section.

 

 

Scroll to find the Query Technical Name (e.g., 2CCMLBALSUM). Remove the 2C prefix and search for this in ABAP Development Tools (ADT) for Eclipse.

 

Bonus Tip: BEx Queries

Sometimes, you might encounter a BEx Query in the Configuration section of the Fiori Apps Library. Don’t be deterred; this means you’re close. Remove the 2C prefix from the query name and search for the corresponding CDS view in ADT.

 

Conclusion

There is no single, direct path from an Analytical Fiori App to a CDS view. However, by following these three approaches—Library documentation, linked Help pages, and App Configuration—you can confidently find what you need. I hope these methods assist you as they have helped me.

entry.title: SAP Sustainability Control Tower: Q3-Q4 2024 Updates & Highlights
entry.content:
Co-Authors: @bilgeakdur @amandamasuku 

Welcome to the future of ESG Reporting and Management with SAP Sustainability Control Tower! This blog post will explore the latest developments in the SAP Sustainability Control Tower and emphasize its crucial role in advancing your Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) agenda.

Positioned at the forefront of sustainability solutions, SAP Sustainability Control Tower is an ERP-centric, cloud-based platform designed to seamlessly integrate with SAP S/4HANA Cloud and other SAP solutions, including SAP Datasphere, SAP EHS Management, SAP Sustainability Footprint Management and SAP Analytics Cloud. These integration capabilities ensure sustainability is embedded into core business processes, providing automated, auditable, and compliant reporting for ESG data. 

Leveraging AI-driven features, the platform also automates the generation of comprehensive ESG reports and enables businesses to make informed decisions. 

With a strategic focus on expanding regulatory coverage and facilitating integration across solutions, SAP Sustainability Control Tower empowers organizations to achieve their sustainability goals while gaining a competitive advantage in the market.

The following features play a central role in the reporting capabilities solution. Initially, the ESG Data Foundation within the SAP Sustainability Control Tower offers a predefined data model designed to comply with regulatory standards by using pre-built and custom-built sustainability metrics. The solution is transitioning from supporting voluntary reports such as World Economic Forum (WEF) metrics to adopting ESRS and EU Taxonomy, which can also support customers who are starting to address SEC regulations and ISSB standards. Concurrently, this data foundation can also be used for Corporate Carbon Footprint (CCF) calculation and GHG reporting. 

Stay tuned as we explore the key features and benefits of the SAP Sustainability Control Tower:

  • Carbon Management Features of SAP SCT
  • Generate ESG Reports with AI
  • Disclosure Management Strategy
  • Data Foundation and Extensibility
  • Metric Management
  • Auditability
  • SAP Continuous Influence Program
  • Planned Feature Updates
  • Further Assets

 

Carbon Management Features of SAP SCT

As of June 2024, SCT's corporate carbon management capabilities enable you to accurately calculate both direct and indirect emissions, ensuring compliance and aiding in decarbonization efforts across your operations and supply chains. You can efficiently manage data collection across organizational boundaries and track the completion of collection tasks. 

Our solution allows for the calculation of all categories of corporate emissions, including scope 1, 2, and all sub-categories of scope 3. It supports large-scale calculations based on spending and activities. Additionally, you can leverage an emissions factors library, which includes pre-delivered EPA content. Notably, corporate carbon footprint calculations are a built-in feature of SCT, requiring no additional products or licenses.

With the new Corrective Bookings feature, you can now correct past emissions records within the Emissions DPI. Each correction is fully traceable, displaying the original data alongside the corrected values and the period of correction. This ensures transparency and compliance for auditing purposes.

 

Generate ESG Reports with AI

As of August 2024, you can generate draft reports for periodic internal or external ESG reporting, based on the ESG data available in the SAP Sustainability Control Tower. 

The “Generate ESG Reports with AI” functionality utilizes best-practice templates, generative AI text, and metrics calculated in SAP SCT using the OpenAI GPT 4 model via the SAP AI core service. 

In the first version, users can choose from pre-defined SAP templates. With the planned deliveries in Q4/2024, they will soon be able to edit the templates based on their requirements. Draft reports can be distributed within the organization for reviews, collaboration, and adaptive iterations. 

 

Disclosure Management Strategy

Due to the CSRD disclosure requirements, companies have to also report on how sustainability issues affect their business and the impact of their activities on people and the environment. These requirements are detailed in the European Sustainability Reporting Standards (ESRS), which require a formal disclosure and exchange of disclosure documents via XBRL format with the authorities.

SAP Sustainability Control Tower aggregates, plans, calculates and steers ESG factors and goals.  Across the world, companies strive to improve their environmental, social, and governance (ESG) metrics and disclose them combined with financial data in their integrated reports. SAP SCT can distribute relevant data into existing disclosure management tools (SAP or non-SAP) to generate integrated reporting. 

Our ambition is to share the ESG metrics available in SAP SCT with disclosure management software to enable the creation, validation, and filing or disclosure of regulatory reports in Word, XBRL, and iXBRL formats, including ESRS. 

Therefore, Thomson Reuters will launch an ESG Disclosure Management app (target availability in Q1/2025), which extends SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SAP SCT) to enable customers with a software solution to simplify their ESG reporting and filing needs. This app will integrate SAP SCT with Thomson Reuters' ONESOURCE Statutory Reporting (TR OSR) to provide customers with last-mile reporting functionality based on the SAP SCT data foundation.

Moreover, the upcoming release of SAP Disclosure Management (target availability in Q4/2024) we release SCT data source connection to seamlessly integrate ESG data between the two solutions to save time and ensure ESG data consistency for existing customers.

 

Data Foundation and Extensibility

As we continue to evolve the SAP Sustainability Control Tower, a key focus is enhancing the extensibility of the ESG Data Foundation. This allows organizations to tailor the platform to their unique sustainability reporting needs, ensuring comprehensive and accurate ESG data management.

We are pleased to introduce a new custom metric type: Standard Custom Metrics. This feature allows you to define calculations directly within the Manage Metrics app and perform them in the Manage ESG Data app, using methods such as sum, division, and weighted division.

With this enhancement, we have updated the names of existing custom metrics to better reflect their functionalities:

  • Basic Metric is now Basic Custom Metric: Create and configure your metric in the Manage Metrics app without requiring measures for the calculation. There is no change in functionality.
  • Advanced Metric is now Legacy Advanced Custom Metric: Metrics and calculation methods previously defined in the Manage Metrics app and calculated in CPE. No new metrics of this type can be created.
  • Standard Custom Metric: Newly introduced, allowing you to define your calculation methodology directly in the Manage Metrics app and calculate in the Manage ESG Data app.

These changes enhance the flexibility and precision of your sustainability metrics, enabling more tailored reporting and analysis.

To streamline your data import processes, you can now import data for Basic Custom Metrics directly using CSV files in the Custom Metrics - Actuals tab of the Manage ESG Data app. Templates are readily available within the app to guide you through the process. This update eliminates the need for the CPE environment for this function, ensuring a more efficient workflow without affecting your existing data.

As of October 2024, we’ve expanded the import and export capabilities to include custom metrics alongside custom measures and dimensions. This functionality is available within the Manage Metrics, Manage Measures, and Manage Custom Dimensions apps. You can now export a set of custom objects and import them into another tenant, facilitating easier data transfer and system setup across different environments. For a comprehensive guide on this process, refer to Exporting and Importing Custom Objects.

To facilitate data import processes, you can now use Microsoft Excel (XLSX/XLS) files in addition to CSV files when importing data in the Manage Master Data and Manage ESG Data apps. The system automatically adapts to typical date formats in Excel files, enhancing ease of use and reducing data formatting issues.

To offer greater customization, dimensions with SAP-provided values now have either Read Only or Active status in the Manage Master Data app. You can add new values to dimensions with Active status, allowing you to seamlessly combine SAP-provided and custom values without creating entirely new custom dimensions.

For example, if the "Contract Type" dimension has four default SAP-provided values and you require an additional value, you can simply add it to the existing dimension. This streamlined approach simplifies dimension management and enhances data consistency.

You can import new master data values via file import or use the new Edit function within the Manage Master Data app.

 

Metric Management

Simplified Creation of Standard Custom Metrics

Defining your calculation methods is now more straightforward. With the Manage Metrics app, you can create Standard Custom Metrics and define your calculation methodology directly within the app, choosing from sum, division, and weighted division methods. This eliminates the need for external calculations and streamlines the metric creation process for many additional metrics ESG standards require.

Assurance Information Configuration

You can now configure the disclosure type, applicable reporting standards, set materiality, and specify the assurance level for each metric in the new Assurance section. This enhancement allows for more detailed reporting and better alignment with regulatory requirements.

 

Auditability

In October, the "View Change Logs" application was released, designed to enhance transparency and compliance with regulatory obligations. This feature allows users to track and review changes across various data categories, including metrics, custom measures, dimensions, and activity data for measures.
